title: Aureline safe-preview trust-class and suspicious-content vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the safe-preview trust-class and suspicious-content vocabulary frozen in docs/security/safe_preview_trust_classes.md. This schema exports surface_trust_resolution_record and suspicious_content_case_record so editor, docs/help, review, install, preview, approval, delete-review, and support/export surfaces can project one trust-class, downgrade, owner/origin, and suspicious-content contract without carrying raw bodies. Trust-class names use the architecture spellings verbatim: RawText, SanitizedRich, TrustedLocalActive, and IsolatedRemoteActive.

trust_class_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the safe-preview trust-class vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

opaque_id.description: Opaque id safe to log, export, or persist in summaries. Raw content, paths, URLs, and secret material never appear here.

surface_family.description: Closed surface-family vocabulary for the safe-preview contract.

origin_kind.description: Closed origin-kind vocabulary naming where the rendered subject came from.

trust_class.description: Frozen safe-preview trust-class vocabulary using the architecture spellings verbatim.

allowed_behavior.description: Closed allowed-behavior vocabulary. A record names only behaviors currently admissible for the resolved trust class.

representation_action_id.description: Transfer-safe representation action ids re-exported from the text representation policy contract.

upgrade_trigger.description: Closed upgrade-trigger vocabulary. A richer trust class may be entered only when all required triggers hold.

downgrade_trigger.description: Closed downgrade-trigger vocabulary. A trigger narrows the surface toward raw inspection, static snapshot, or metadata-only.

downgrade_posture.description: Closed downgrade-posture vocabulary describing the effective narrowing path after guarantees are lost.

connectivity_state.description: Closed connectivity-state vocabulary used by active and downgraded remote/embedded surfaces.

required_chrome_field.description: Closed owner/origin chrome vocabulary. Compact layouts may rearrange these fields but may not hide them at commit.

badge_label.description: Representative trust-class or warning badge label shown on the primary surface.

warning_label.description: Optional representative warning label for suspicious-content cases.

label_examples.description: Representative user-facing label set that keeps trust-class and transfer posture explicit.

annotation_mode.description: Closed annotation-mode vocabulary. strict_trust_decision applies on install/publish/attach; approval_or_delete_review preserves the last visible evidence path before commit.

location_kind.description: Closed location-kind vocabulary for suspicious-content findings.

visibility_impact.description: Closed visibility-impact vocabulary naming how the suspicious content can mislead a human reader.

reveal_affordance.description: Closed reveal-affordance vocabulary. Findings on trust-decision surfaces use primary-surface affordances rather than tooltip-only disclosure.

reveal_affordances.description: Primary-surface reveal affordances made available for this finding.

notes.description: Optional reviewable note summarising the observed risk or required operator action.

suspicious_content_finding_record.description: Concrete suspicious-content finding attached to a surface.

effective_allowed_behaviors.description: Behaviors currently admissible for the surface in its resolved trust class.

default_transfer_actions.description: Transfer-safe copy/export actions surfaced as defaults for the resolved trust class.

upgrade_trigger_requirements.description: Conditions that must hold before this surface may enter a richer trust class.

downgrade_trigger_observations.description: Observed or relevant triggers that would narrow this surface.

required_owner_origin_chrome.description: Chrome fields that must remain visible on the primary surface.

related_finding_refs.description: Optional ids of suspicious-content findings attached to this trust resolution.

notes.description: Optional reviewable note explaining why the surface holds or narrows to this trust class.

surface_trust_resolution_record.description: Resolved safe-preview state for one concrete surface and subject.

findings.description: One or more suspicious-content findings attached to the surface.

stricter_annotation_requirements.description: Additional requirements that apply because the surface is making a trust, approval, or delete-review decision.

notes.description: Optional note describing the required operator guidance or review expectation.

suspicious_content_case_record.description: Worked suspicious-content case attached to a concrete surface.
